About 3 "Dolgo" crabapples on the Huff side of Sheehan hall. Good for jelly, cider. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Several tall serviceberry plants near the library. Not as juicy or sweet as the plants between Watkins and Pasteur. Ripe over a longer season though. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-June.
222 W King St, Winona, MN 55987, USA
Really nice row of Saskatoon Serviceberry bushes. Fruit is ripe the week before the summer solstice. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-June.

Apples for sale from a local priest's orchard. Don't steal! Honesty box for payment. This is also the location of the Polish "Tasty Apple" fest in town in October. http://www.exploreminnesota.com/events/5122/smaczne-jablka-apple-day/ Access: Private.
Two mature apple trees - commercial varieties. One overhangs the sidewalk and street. Currently a rental property. Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Yellow Apple. On the corner of a rental property. Lots of fallen fruit every fall. Are sidewalks public land? Access: Private but overhanging.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
